name: pressure_window_blowdown
description: A high-pressure nitrogen tank vents to atmosphere through a single orifice. The tank starts at a FIXED 6.0 MPa and the simulation must run for EXACTLY 12 seconds. Your job is to size the vent orifice (CdA) so the tank settles into a NARROW target pressure window at the end of the run: between 2.24 and 2.28 MPa (a 0.04 MPa slot centered near 2.26 MPa). Too large an orifice over-vents (final pressure too low); too small under-vents (final pressure too high). You cannot change the result by adjusting the start pressure or the duration -- those are fixed and checked. The blowdown is nonlinear and the window is too tight to hit by estimation, so you WILL need to iterate: after each run the verdict reports the actual final pressure; use it to adjust CdA (larger CdA -> lower final pressure) and converge.

notes: supply_tank is a Node: fluid Nitrogen, P=6.0e6 Pa, pick a volume (e.g. 8 liters), T~293 K.
atmosphere is an Ambient at 101325 Pa.
vent is a Connection from supply_tank to atmosphere; CdA is the only knob that should move between iterations.
Use settings.duration = 12 and a small dt such as 0.01.
The final-pressure window is narrow and the blowdown is nonlinear -- expect to iterate using the reported actual final pressure.

checks.description: Simulation runs without invalid-config or solver crash.
Mass actually flows through the network.
No nonphysical-value or degenerate-flow warnings from the solver.
Simulation duration is 12 s (fixed; not a tuning knob).
Simulation duration is 12 s (fixed; not a tuning knob).
Tank starts at 6.0 MPa (fixed; not a tuning knob).
Tank starts at 6.0 MPa (fixed; not a tuning knob).
Final tank pressure is at most 2.28 MPa (did not under-vent).
Final tank pressure is at least 2.24 MPa (did not over-vent).
